title: Short, hooky title — plain English, no jargon
tldr: One punchy sentence — the single takeaway, the way you'd text it to a friend.

sections.body: What problem or curiosity drove the study. Set the stage in plain language. ~60-110 words.
The method, explained like you're talking to a smart friend with no science background. Define every term on first use.
The result. Be accurate; describe uncertain figures qualitatively. Never fabricate a number.
Why this mattered / still matters / what it unlocked.
The honest limitation, caveat, replication status, or nuance. (Mice vs humans? Small sample? Later trials?)

takeaways: Short, punchy line a reader can actually use or remember.
Third — optionally connect to the reader (a curious finance/AI builder).

quiz.q: A clear question that tests understanding, not trivia.
quiz.why: Explain why the right answer is right — teach in the explanation.
